paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: The four largest global markets among selected agricultural products are Shrimp ($15 billion), Plantains & Bananas ($14 billion), Cuttlefish/Octopus & Bivalves ($11 billion), and Tomatoes ($8.5 billion). However, market size and growth don't always align. Shrimp stands out as both the largest and fastest-growing product globally. Plantains & Bananas also have a very large market with moderate growth. Cuttlefish/Octopus & Bivalves have a large market growing at a moderately fast pace. Tomatoes, despite their large market, show low overall growth — with the EU market for tomatoes actually shrinking. This means Shrimp and Plantains offer the most attractive combination of scale and momentum, while Tomatoes represent a mature, slower-growth opportunity.
supporting_quotes: Largest markets are: Shrimp (15 Bn, Plantains 14 Bn, Cuttlefish/Bivalves 11 Bn, and Tomatoes 8.5 Bn)
Shrimp: Growing fastest and has a very large world market
Tomatoes: Large world market but low growth (moderate in US and shrinking in EU)
Plantains & Bananas: Growing moderately fast with a very large world market
